UTAH DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION REPORTS THAT BOTH DIRECTIONS OF US HIGHWAY 6 CLOSED AT MP 292 FOR AROUND 45 MINUTES WEDNESDAY EVENING AS NEARBY HEAVY RAIN CAUSED WATER MUD AND RAILROAD TIES FROM AN ADJACENT RAILROAD TO FLOW ONTO THE HIGHWAY. RADAR ESTIMES SUGGEST 1 TO 2 INCHES OF RAIN HAD FALLEN NEARBY. MEDIA REPORTS THAT A FAMILYS SUV WAS WASHED OFF THE ROAD AND THE FAMILY REQUIRED RESCUE. NO INJURIES WERE REPORTED.

TOOELE COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED A DEBRIS FLOW MOVING OFF OF THE JACOB CITY BURN SCAR FROM SOLDIER CANYON. THICK DEBRIS MOVED OFF THE BURN SCAR ACROSS THE CANYON ROAD AND INTO SOLDIER CREEK. THE FLOW CONTINUED DOWN THE CREEK THREATENING THREE HOMES, WHICH WERE EVACULATED.
